diet_advice.current_diet_assessment: Daily soft drink consumption is the biggest dietary problem, directly contributing to high blood sugar.
priority_changes: Strictly eliminate all sugary soft drinks and aerated beverages.
Increase consistent daily intake of fruits and vegetables for fiber and micronutrients.
Focus on portion control of carbohydrate-rich foods at all meals.
Ensure regular intake of protein through dal, eggs, or paneer.
diet_advice.breakfast: 2 small Wheat Dosa with 1 cup Sambar and 1 boiled egg — Provides balanced carbs, protein, and vegetables; affordable and easy to prepare.
diet_advice.lunch: 1 cup Hand-pounded Rice with 1 cup Mixed Vegetable Sambar and 1 cup curried seasonal vegetables — Offers complex carbs, protein, fiber, and supports steady blood sugar.
diet_advice.dinner: 2 small Whole Wheat Chapatis with 1 cup Dal and 1 cup green leafy vegetable curry — Light, high-fiber, protein-rich meal that is easy to digest.
foods_to_avoid: Soft drinks — high in sugar, directly elevates blood glucose and HbA1c.
Deep-fried snacks (e.g., samosa, pakora) — high in unhealthy fats, contributing to CKM risks.
Packaged biscuits/sweets — often contain hidden sugars and refined flours, detrimental to glycemic control.
diet_advice.clinical_note: Patient's HbA1c has significantly worsened to 8.3%, coinciding with no reported oral antidiabetic medications; urgent review of medication adherence and regimen is needed. Also, flag the extremely low sleep duration (4 hours).
